column1: No. of PatientsDENGUE CASE REPORTING During  the  24th  week,  4824  suspected  dengue  cases were  reported  across  25  districts,  compared  to  3255 cases in the  previous  week, reflecting  an  48.2% increase. The Western Province accounted for 55.4% of total cases, with the Colombo Municipal Council (CMC) contributing 3.1%, the rest of Colombo District 21.9%, Gampaha  District  21.5%,  and  Kalutara  District  8.8%. Outside the Western Province, notable case contributions were reported from Matara (9.7%), Kandy (7.8%),  Galle  (5.7%),  Ratnapura  (5.1%),  Kurunegala (2.8%), Kegalle (2.2%), and Hambantota (2.1%) (see Table 1). Previously classified 9 high-risk MOH areas in week 23, were not identified as high-risk in the 24th week of 2026 (Table 2). MIDNIGHT TOTAL OF DENGUE PATIENT ADMISSIONS *  After  incorporating  delayed  hospital  reports,  the average midnight total for the 23rd week increased from 1185 to 1189. In the 24th week of 2026, the average midnight total of dengue patients across  74 sentinel hospitals was 1590. Compared  to  the  previous  week,  in  the  24th  week,  40 hospitals  reported  an increase,  while  16  reported  a decrease.
